sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: IBM Expands Entry-Level Hiring Even as AI Reshapes Work
feed.summary: IBM plans to triple its entry-level hiring in 2026, redefining junior roles to work alongside AI rather than being replaced by it. The company is rehauling job descriptions, particularly in software development and human resources, to focus on strategic tasks that leverage human insight while AI handles routine operations. This strategy counters concerns about job displacement and aims to build a future talent pipeline.

feed.title: United States Food Waste Management Market 2026 | Market with
feed.summary: The Food Waste Management Market reached US$69.8 billion in 2024 and is projected to grow to US$136.2 billion by 2032, driven by increasing global food waste, stringent government regulations, environmental awareness, and technological advancements. The report highlights recent industry developments in the United States and Japan, key players, and segmented market analysis by waste type, source, service, and process. North America currently holds the largest share, followed by Europe and Asia-Pacific, with growth fueled by circular economy solutions and sustainability pushes.

feed.title: McDonald’s invests in deforestation-free soy initiative to strengthen sustainable sourcing
feed.summary: McDonald's has joined the Responsible Commodities Facility (RCF) to invest in deforestation-free and conversion-free soy production in Brazil’s Cerrado, specifically for chicken feed used outside the US and Canada. The investment aims to strengthen the company's sustainable sourcing strategy, protect critical ecosystems, and reduce supply chain risks. This initiative reflects the significance of chicken on McDonald's global menu, making the soy in poultry feed a key sustainability focus, with other major investors contributing to the RCF as well.

feed.title: Zenas BioPharma Highlights Obexelimab Phase 3 Win, Targets Q2 BLA Filing at Guggenheim Summit
feed.summary: Zenas BioPharma highlighted positive Phase 3 results for obexelimab in IgG4-related disease (IgG4-RD), showing a 56% reduction in flare risk and strong durability in early open-label extension data. The company plans a U.S. BLA filing in Q2 and a European filing in H2, anticipating obexelimab could become a significantly greater than $1 billion drug in the U.S. Zenas also provided updates on its lupus, progressive MS, and earlier-stage pipeline programs at the Guggenheim Emerging Outlook Biotech Summit.

feed.title: MrBeast Wades In After Grandson of Reese’s Cup Inventor Calls Out Hershey’s
feed.summary: YouTube star and entrepreneur MrBeast has joined a public dispute regarding Reese's Peanut Butter Cups after the inventor's grandson criticized Hershey's for allegedly diluting the brand's ingredients. Brad Reese accused The Hershey Company of replacing milk chocolate and real peanut butter with alternative ingredients in some products. MrBeast, who launched his own chocolate brand Feastables in 2022, shared Reese's message, implying that Feastables' peanut butter cups were created in response to these perceived quality changes.

feed.title: Verizon (VZ) Stock; Slips Ahead of Presidents Day as Insider Share Sales Surface

Investors are now looking to upcoming investor events for clarity on Verizon's strategic outlook amid these short-term pressures.

feed.title: Brown & Brown sees improving conditions for prepared buyers in 2026 market trends
feed.summary: Brown & Brown's 2026 Market Trends report indicates improving conditions for insurance buyers who demonstrate strong risk management, though some sectors and coverages remain challenging. The report, which includes insights from Risk Strategies, highlights increased competition in many commercial segments due to added capacity while also noting the impact of large-loss events on underwriter behavior. It also touches on managing employee benefits costs with digital tools and the influence of climate volatility and regulation on personal lines.

feed.title: Intel backs away from manufacturing deal with Tower Semiconductor
feed.summary: Intel has informed Tower Semiconductor that it does not intend to proceed with their previously signed agreement for Intel to manufacture 300mm wafers for Tower's customers. The two companies are currently in a mediation process, and affected customers are being redirected to Tower's Fab 7 facility in Japan. This development was revealed alongside Tower Semiconductor's announcement of record fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results.
